2.title: Supernova in the Pinwheel galaxy
2.content: Supernova in the Pinwheel galaxy (Messier 101 or NGC 5457) called SN 2023ixf has been discovered by Koichi Itagaki on 19 May 2023.1 . Located 21 million light years away, it's confirmed to be a type II supernova.2 . | | . Supernova in the Pinwheel galaxy taken from my backyard 6 days after being discovered.3 . References . | Discovery certificate for object 2023ixf. Transient Name Server. &#8617; . | AstroNote 2023-119. Transient Name Server. &#8617; . | SN 2023ixf. Flickr. &#8617; . | .

3.title: Sunspots 8 July 2023
3.content: . | | . Photo of the Sun captured from my backyard on 8 July 2023.1 There are 8 active regions (sunspots) visible on its Earth-facing side (from AR3359 to AR3366).2 . References . | Sun 2023-07-08. Flickr. &#8617; . | Sun activity: 3 sunspot regions hold promise. Earthsky. &#8617; . | .

4.title: Sunspots 17 July 2023
4.content: . | | . Photo of the Sun captured on 17 July 2023.1 There are 6 active regions (sunspots) visible on its Earth-facing side (AR3363 and from AR3371 to AR3375).2 . References . | Sun 2023-07-17. Flickr. &#8617; . | Sun activity: CME glancing blow headed our way. Earthsky. &#8617; . | .
